label: Moving Expenses

primary.description: Moving expense deduction for eligible relocations

plain_language: If you moved at least 40km closer to a new job, business, or school, you may deduct eligible moving costs against income earned at the new location.

reasons.condition_not_asked: Distance could not be confirmed in this interview
reasons.fact_dependent: Deduction ceiling depends on income earned at new location

limits.description: Deductible to the extent of income earned at the new location
limits.carryforward_note: Unused portion carries forward to following tax year under s.62(1)(c)

eligible_expenses: Legal fees and land transfer taxes on new home
Cost of maintaining vacant former residence

supporting_documents.note: Offer letter T4 or business registration
If home was sold or purchased as part of move
If carryforward from prior year suspected

preparer_flags.flag: Distance measured by shortest public road route — confirm calculation method
Income at new location determines deduction ceiling
Check 2024 NOA for any moving expense carryforward

legislation_interaction.note: Deduction limited to employment or business income earned at new location

planning_notes: If income at new location is low in year of move carryforward maximizes the deduction in the following year.

newcomer_relevance.note: Newcomers relocating for first Canadian employment frequently miss this deduction
